title: Butter on Polyester
stainChemistry: Butter is ~80% butterfat + small casein + salt. Polyester is OLEOPHILIC — grabs butterfat tenaciously. Two ordering rules: (1) cold flush BEFORE warm Dawn handles the small casein fraction first, (2) NO hot water until fully clear (heat sets butterfat into polyester permanently AND yellows the fiber over time). Standard butter protocol with polyester-specific Dawn × 2 if oily feel remains.
whyThisWorks: Cold water removes casein fraction. Warm Dawn emulsifies butterfat (Dawn is engineered for triglyceride). Cool wash + air-dry until fully clear preserves polyester. Polyester typically needs Dawn × 2 because of the oleophilic affinity.
defaultAssumption: Polyester garment / blend / kid clothes. Fresh butter = quick win. Hot-dried = harder; sometimes permanent.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Scrape solid butter off with a dull blade. Lift bulk only.
Flip inside out. Cold water through the BACK. Handles small casein fraction before warm Dawn touches the fat.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dawn + warm (not hot) water, soft toothbrush
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dawn in warm water (NOT hot — heat sets butterfat into polyester). Soft toothbrush 5 min. Cool rinse.

spottingProtocol.agent: Repeat Dawn If Oily Feel Remains
spottingProtocol.instruction: Test with clean fingertip. Oily feel = repeat Dawn. Polyester typically needs 2 passes.
Cool wash. Air-dry. Inspect BEFORE dryer — heat sets butterfat into polyester permanently.

steps: 3. Dawn + soft toothbrush warm water (not hot), 5 min.
4. Repeat Dawn pass if oily feel remains.
5. Cool wash + air-dry. Inspect before dryer.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold flush + warm Dawn (not hot) + cool wash
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets butterfat into polyester + yellows fiber
NEVER chlorine bleach on polyester — yellows fiber
NEVER hot dryer until fully clear
Polyester is oleophilic — expect 2 Dawn passes
Cold flush BEFORE warm Dawn — casein first

homeSolutions.agent: Polyester + Butter — Cold First, Then Warm Dawn
homeSolutions.instruction: Butter has a small milk-protein fraction (casein) — cold flush handles that first. Polyester is oleophilic (grabs oil) — Dawn × 2 typical for the butterfat. Two rules: NO hot water (sets butterfat into polyester permanently) and NO chlorine bleach (yellows polyester).
Scrape solid butter off with a dull butter knife. Just lift the bulk.
Flip inside out. Cold tap water through the BACK 1 minute. Cold flush handles the small casein fraction before warm water touches the fat.

homeSolutions.agent: Dawn + Warm (Not Hot) Water — Soft Toothbrush 5 Minutes
homeSolutions.instruction: Dawn in WARM water — not hot, just warm enough to emulsify butterfat. Work into spot with soft toothbrush 5 min. Cool rinse.

homeSolutions.agent: Test for Oily Feel — Repeat Dawn If Yes
homeSolutions.instruction: Touch the spot with a clean fingertip. Still feels oily? Repeat Dawn. Polyester typically needs 2 passes.
Cool wash per care label. Air-dry. Inspect BEFORE dryer. Residual butterfat + dryer heat = permanent on polyester.

steps: 1. Cool water only. NO hot water until fully clear.
3. Cold water back-flush — casein step first.
4. Dawn + WARM (not hot) water + soft toothbrush, 5 min.
5. Repeat Dawn if oily feel remains.
6. Cool wash + AIR-DRY. Inspect before dryer.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot dryer was used on residual butterfat (likely permanent on polyester).
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets butterfat + yellows polyester
NEVER hot dryer until fully clear
Polyester loves oil — expect 2 Dawn passes

neverDo: Never hot water on butter + polyester
Never hot dryer until fully clear
fiberSensitivities: Polyester yellows under heat over time
Polyester typically needs 2 Dawn passes for oil

materialWarnings: No hot water / hot dryer — heat sets butterfat
Polyester loves oil — expect 2 Dawn passes

escalation.when: Hot dryer used on residual butterfat, alkaline applied, or 3 home rounds plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Butter on polyester, age [hours / days], home-treated with cold flush + Dawn warm water × 2 + cool wash + air-dry. No hot water / no chlorine bleach. [Residual oily feel]."

contextQuestions: How long ago — fresh / dried in / weeks?
Has it been through a hot wash or dryer?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Compound butter (garlic, herb) adds minor pigment that may need a 3% peroxide step on white polyester. Hot-dried butterfat = often permanent on polyester.
